text: Objective: To study the effects of the aqueous extract of Prangos ferulacea plant on the behavior of the foodborne pathogenic bacteria Listeria monocytogenes and on the survival of human colorectal cancer cells (HT29). Methods: For evaluation of the biological effects of the aqueous extract of the P. ferulacea plant, initially, the extract was prepared by the maceration method. Subsequently, the total phenolic content, flavonoid content and beta-carotene of the plant extract were measured and the antioxidant activity was investigated using DPPH, ABTS free radical scavenging and FRAP assay. The effects of aqueous extract of P. ferulacea on cancerous cells (human colorectal cancer cells; HT 29) were investigated by means of IC50. The antibacterial effects of aqueous extract of P. ferulacea were examined by disk diffusion agar (DDA), well diffusion agar (WDA), minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) and minimum bactericidal concentration (MBC) assays. Scanning electron microscope (SEM) and confocal laser scanning microscopy (CLSM) was applied to observe the morphological changes on L. monocytogenes bacterial cells. The gene expression associated with biofilm formation of L. monocytogenes was also assessed. Results: According to the results obtained from HPLC test, p-coumaric acid was identified as the main component of the P. ferulacea extract using FTIR test and revealed the presence of the characteristic functional groups of phenolic, flavonoid, tannins and carboxylic acids in this extract such as caffeic acid and coumaric acid. Total phenol content (TPC), total flavonoid content (TFC), and beta-carotene of the plant extract were 202.04 ± 5.46 mg gallic acid equivalent (GAE)/g dry weight, 1,909.46 ± 13 μg quercetin (QE)/g of dry weight, and 2.91 mg/100 g, respectively. The antioxidant activity of P. ferulacea extract was determined using 2,2-Diphenyl-1-picrylhydrazyl (DPPH) and 2,2′-azino-bis (3-ethylbenzothiazoline-6-sulphonic acid (ABTS) assays. The IC50 value for DDPH and ABTS was 274 ± 7.2 μg/mL and 120.45 ± 9.6 μg/mL, respectively. In addition, the FRAP value was 1.92 ± 0.05 μg ascorbic acid equivalent (AAE)/g of extract. It was observed that IC50 of the extract on cancerous cells was 82.15 ± 0.02 μg/mL. The antibacterial effects of aqueous extract of P. ferulacea were investigated using DDA and WDA assays. The results of the inhibitory zone of aqueous extract of P. ferulacea against the tested bacteria using the DDA method were 21±1.03 mm and 18±0.85 mm, respectively, while the results of inhibitory zone of aqueous extract of P. ferulacea against the tested bacteria using the WDA method were 14±1.33 mm and 12±0.91 mm, respectively. The MIC and MBC values of the aqueous extract of P. ferulacea on Listeria monocytogenes were 16 and 128 mg/mL, respectively. The morphology change of L. monocytogenes strain was proved through SEM and CLSM microscopes. In addition, the extract caused a significant reduction in the transcription of genes involved in the film formation ability of L. monocytogenes . Conclusion: The obtained results fully prove the very practical and pragmatic characteristics of P. ferulacea .
